title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves fourth place on LM Arena's open model leaderboard while running on a single GPU, requiring half to a quarter the compute of comparable competitors. The model supports deployment on phones, multiple GPUs for hospital systems, and multi-agent workloads on consumer hardware via LM Studio, with an Apache 2.0 license enabling deployment in sovereign contexts without extended procurement delays.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma 4's 31B model, which reaches fourth place on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU rather than the four or five required by comparable models. The talk frames efficiency as a path to ownership: the model runs on resource-constrained hardware including Pixel phones and Apple Silicon, with medical variants operating on two GPUs. The shift to Apache 2.0 licensing is the key enabler, allowing sovereign deployment in regulated jurisdictions.
